stories.title: Macron praises Europe’s predictability in face of countries that ‘hurt you without even informing you’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed Meanwhile, the European Union has sought to ramp up pressure on Hungary to drop its veto on the €90bn loan for Ukraine, with the European Commission saying it will push ahead with its preparatory work for the loan to be paid out. The commission said it would draft a legal text setting out the details of the first payment of €45bn in 2026 and what the funds would be used for, and send it to the European Council to be formally approved by the bloc’s 27 leaders. “ We pr

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Russia responds to Zelenskyy’s Easter truce offer with drone attack
stories.summary: Ukrainian president says ceasefire could show diplomacy works, while Russia dismisses statement as ‘PR stunt’. What we know on day 1,499 Volodymyr Zelenskyy has criticised Russia for responding to an offer of an Easter truce with airstrikes . The Ukrainian president said on Wednesday he had spoken to US negotiators about an Easter ceasefire but Russian forces had fired more than 700 drones – many of them Iranian-designed Shaheds – targeting parts of western and central Ukraine in a rare daytime

stories.title: Trump’s rage at NATO allies is binding them together — against him
stories.summary: LONDON — Donald Trump’s anger at NATO allies for refusing to join the war against Iran has so far achieved one thing: uniting them against him.  In private, over intimate dinners, and on the sidelines of meetings in Brussels and elsewhere, European leaders and officials are discussing how to handle the U.S. president’s threats to quit NATO and what they would do if he followed through.  They now share the grim view that Trump’s increasingly angry attacks on Britain, Spain, France

stories.title: EU wants Hungary’s next leader to support Ukraine. It should be so lucky.
stories.summary: BUDAPEST — Péter Magyar, the front-runner in Hungary’s upcoming election, shows little sign of being the pro-Ukraine alternative many in Brussels are hoping for. EU leaders, increasingly frustrated with Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s obstruction of support for Kyiv, are quietly betting a Magyar victory on April 12 could reset relations — or at least ease tensions with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. But on substance, Magyar often sounds strikingly similar to the man he’s trying to

stories.title: The Guardian view on Ukraine’s perilous spring: Europe’s steadfast support is more vital than ever | Editorial
stories.summary: As the world’s attention is drawn to the Middle East, Donald Trump’s assault on Iran has had damaging knock-on consequences for Kyiv After meeting European Union foreign ministers this week, Volodymyr Zelenskyy voiced exasperation over the continued blocking of a €90bn EU loan to Kyiv by Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán. US financial support for Ukraine has dried up under Donald Trump, so the money is desperately required. But as Mr Zelenskyy bitterly observed, it is being delayed “because

stories.title: France advising Bahrain on UN resolution to open Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: France is advising Bahrain on a draft United Nations Security Council resolution that would authorize the use of force to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore global energy flows, according to three diplomats informed of the process. The draft resolution — currently being discussed by France, the United States and Gulf countries in New York — would seek the Security Council’s backing for an operation to “restore international peace and security” under Chapter 7 of the U.N. C

stories.title: Rival teams of vote observers spell trouble in Hungarian election
stories.summary: BUDAPEST — The emergence of alternative electoral monitoring groups — including a team created by allies of Prime Minister Viktor Orbán — is raising the prospect of political turmoil and contested results after Hungary’s general election on April 12. Concerns are already running high that the election result will be appealed by whoever loses, given that the campaign has been marked by smear tactics and accusations of foreign interference — something that only increases the importance of vote obs

stories.title: Macron lauds Europe's 'predictability' on visit to Japan in apparent swipe at Trump
stories.summary: Macron's office had expressed surprise at Trump's criticism of France for not letting planes carrying military supplies for the conflict fly over its territory.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán has the support of both Russia and the US. Here’s why that doesn’t seem to be doing him any good | Péter Krekó
stories.summary: In Hungary, the Iran war is exposing tensions. On April 12, voters may decide that Orban’s geopolitical contortions are a liability On 3 March, Viktor Orbán held a phone conversation with Vladimir Putin. According to official Hungarian reporting, the discussion focused on “energy issues” and other routine matters. What followed was anything but routine. Within days, the Hungarian foreign minister, Péter Szijjártó , had flown to Moscow, and returned with two freed prisoners of war , dual citizens

stories.title: ‘God squad’ waives endangered species law to allow US drilling in Gulf of Mexico
stories.summary: Critics say exemption for fossil fuels exploits White House’s ‘self-made gas crisis’, and could doom the rare Rice’s whale Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A US government panel on Tuesday exempted oil and gas drilling in the Gulf of Mexico from the Endangered Species Act (ESA), a move which critics say could doom a rare whale species and harm other marine life . The Endangered Species Committee – which had not convened in more than three decades – vo

stories.title: Red shoes protest in Romania highlights femicide crisis
stories.summary: Red shoes at Bucharest's Romanian Athenaeum honour women killed by domestic violence, as Romania adopts its first femicide law and tougher penalties.

stories.title: Peter Magyar, the former Orban ally vying for power in Hungary
stories.summary: Magyar represents the biggest threat to Viktor Orban's rule in Hungary since he won the first of four consecutive victories in 2010.

stories.title: Nem feltétlenül kell leállítani a Barátság-vezetéket ahhoz, hogy felújítsák
stories.summary: Működő vezeték mellett is lehet szivattyút cserélni – állítják a hazai szakemberek. Az, hogy az ukránok hajlandóak költeni a Barátság-vezetékre, azt jelzi, hogy hosszú távon is terveznek vele.

stories.title: A Chelsea a Premier League történetének legnagyobb veszteségét hozta össze az előző szezonban
stories.summary: 65 millió fonttal szárnyalták túl a Manchester City eddigi rekordját, pedig állításuk szerint a bevételük is majdnem rekordmagas lett.

stories.title: A fideszes jelölt csak ámul és bámul, Csepel exfideszes polgármestere mégis neki hálás mindenért
stories.summary: A kampányban saját, közpénzzel is támogatott civil szervezetét is bevető Király Nóra még azon is meglepődött, hogy Csepelen van mozi. Persze hivatalosan is csak annyi köze van a kerülethez, hogy ő lett ott a Fidesz képviselőjelöltje. A polgármester mégis mindent neki köszön, legyen az új Lidl, felújított rendelő, vagy akadálymentes játék a Süsü játszótérre.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor a fiataloknak Szentesen: Esetleg meg is köszönhetnétek
stories.summary: Kisebb, fiatalokból álló tiltakozócsoport gyűlt össze a beszéd alatt, akik skandálással és dudálással zavarták meg Orbán Viktor beszédét. A miniszterelnök többször is hozzájuk intézte a szavait.

stories.title: „Ilyenek az ukránok”, kampányolt Szijjártó az egyik legszegényebb választókerületben
stories.summary: Ami egyben az egyik legfideszesebb is. A külügyminiszter Baranya aprófalvas választókerületben, a 4-es számúban erősítette a helyi jelölt kampányát.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely: Budapestnek jól állna egy olimpia
stories.summary: A főpolgármester szerint a város szinte készen áll, de örül, hogy az olimpiarendezés nem a kampány része.

stories.title: 12 éves gyerekkel szórólapoztat a tatárszentgyörgyi fideszes polgármester Magyar Péter szerint, a polgármester szerint ez nem igaz
stories.summary: „Nem gondolja, hogy több évtizedes politikai múlttal gyerekekkel hordatnék szórólapot” – mondta a HVG-nek Szehofner József, akit egy hozzánk is eljutott videó alapján keresett meg a lap.

stories.title: Fideszes politikus mellett huszárruhában oké a gyerek, tiszás videóban viszont még homályosan se villanhat fel
stories.summary: Az NVB csütörtöki ülését is az a kérdés uralta, hogy hogyan jelenhetnek meg kiskorúak a politikusok közösségi médiás posztjaiban. A jelek szerint nem attól függ, hogy hogyan, hanem hogy kiében bukkannak fel.

stories.title: Clearing up the scandal that has cast suspicion on the Hungarian secret service is entirely up to the government
stories.summary: It has been days since Direkt36 published an article about an unusual police and intelligence operation, after which an investigator came forward and claimed that his unit had been pressured by the Hungarian intelligence services to conduct house searches at the homes of two IT specialists linked to the Tisza Party. There are no signs of the scandal abating any time soon, and the information which has come to light so far has become highly politicized. One of the IT specialists involved in the c

stories.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
stories.summary: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National

stories.title: Angliában szívbetegségek megelőzésére is ajánlják a népszerű súlycsökkentő szereket
stories.summary: Azoknak a betegeknek, akiknek a BMI-je 27 felett van, és akiknél magas a szív- és érrendszeri betegségek kockázata.

stories.title: Az oroszbarát tyituski módszer: keménykötésű figurákat küldtek rá az utcán az ellenzékre
stories.summary: Orbán országjárásán is megjelentek azok a kormányközeli kötődésű embereknek, akiknek az ellenzéki hangok elnyomása a feladata. A jelenség emlékeztet arra, ahogy a Janukovics-rezsim emberei, a tyituskik léptek fel az ellenzékkel szemben Ukrajnában, de nálunk nem történt olyan erőszak, ami az oroszbarát rendszerekben általános volt.

stories.title: Leszedik a választási plakátokat a budapesti hidak szerkezetéről
stories.summary: Oszlopokra lehet plakátolni, csak a hidak szerkezeti elemeire nem.

stories.title: Rákosrendezőn 2,5-szer annyi lakás épülhet, mint tavaly Budapesten összesen
stories.summary: A most beígért 10 ezer új lakás jelentősen javíthatná az elmúlt évek siralmas és romló statisztikáit.

stories.title: Moszkva reagált Szijjártó és Lavrov kiszivárgott hangfelvételére
stories.summary: Az orosz külügyi szóvivő örült annak, hogy közzétették a felvételt.

stories.title: Árulónak nevezett Pintér Sándor? Nem biztos, hogy tudja, az állomány mit gondol – Szabó Bence az elmúlt napokról
stories.summary: Miért lepte meg, hogy hogyan fogadták a rendőrségen? Mit gondol arról, hogy Orbán baleknak nevezte? Interjú a Tisza elleni műveletről borító Szabó Bencével.

stories.title: Undorítónak nevezte Szijjártó és Lavrov beszélgetését az ukrán külügyminiszter
stories.summary: Andrij Szibiha nyomozás megindítását sürgette az ügyben.

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia react to leaked Szijjártó-Lavrov calls
stories.summary: Ukraine's Foreign Minister Andriy Sybiha used the words "disgusting" and "disgrace", but the Russian Foreign Ministry's Maria Zakharova had a different opinion.

stories.title: Féltik a szavazataikat: több erdélyi Tisza-szavazó személyesen viszi a borítékját Magyarországra
stories.summary: Egyre többen döntenek úgy Erdélyben, hogy nem adják át szavazatukat az RMDSZ-es közvetítőknek, hogy biztosak legyenek benne, nem vész el vagy nem módosul útközben.

stories.title: Az ír és a német nyelvű irodalom körül forog majd minden a 31. Budapesti Nemzetközi Könyvfesztiválon
stories.summary: Az idei fesztivál díszvendége az ír Colm Tóibín lesz, és a Frankfurti Könyvvásár is csatlakozik a rendezvényhez.

stories.title: „Semmit nem csinálnék máshogy” – mondta a Tisza informatikusai elleni nyomozásról kitálaló Szabó Bence
stories.summary: Az NNI volt nyomozója reagált arra, hogy Orbán Viktor baleknak, Pintér Sándor pedig árulónak nevezte. Azt is elárulta, hogy NAV-os nyomozóként találkozott a költségvetési csalás miatt elítélt Hajdú Péterrel, akit még gyanúsítottként hallgatott ki, és aki most költözött be egy adás erejéig Orbán Viktorhoz.

stories.title: „Harmadosztályú trash celeb, bűnöző” – Hajdú Péter is szóba került Magyar Péter szeghalmi beszédében
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke arra reagált, hogy Szabó Bence a Telex élő műsorában elmondta, a NAV nyomozójaként dolgozott a műsorvezető korábbi adócsalási ügyén.

stories.title: Wolf bites woman in Hamburg shopping area in Germany's first attack in decades
stories.summary: Wolves are now present in almost all EU countries, and their numbers have increased from 11,000 in 2012 to more than 20,000 in 2023. Italy, Bulgaria, Romania and Spain have populations of more than 2,000.

stories.title: Avi Lewis, elected to lead Canada’s New Democratic party, promises ‘NDP comeback’
stories.summary: Ex-TV host pledged to centre party around equity, with higher wealth taxes, green energy and tuition-free education Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Canada’s embattled New Democratic party (NDP) has elected the former broadcaster and self-proclaimed socialist Avi Lewis as its new leader, as it looks to rebuild following a devastating federal election last year that saw it lose official party status. A record number of members voted in the three-day ND

stories.title: In an Istanbul market, I found an old German phrase book – and a reminder of how not to speak to migrants | Carolin Würfel
stories.summary: Turkish immigrants to Germany in the 60s were seen as temporary labour, not people. Today’s government in Berlin is at risk of repeating the mistake A few weekends ago, I went to the flea market in Bomonti, a neighbourhood on the European side of Istanbul. I go there regularly, and over the years I’ve accumulated a small collection of things: embroidered napkins, records, old issues of House & Garden, earrings, candle holders. It is usually on the days when you are not looking for anything i

stories.title: Starmer calls for ‘ambitious’ new UK-EU ties as Trump threatens to quit Nato
stories.summary: PM to focus on European defence and economic partnership for ‘dangerous world’, in pivot away from US UK politics live – latest updates The UK will seek an even deeper partnership with the EU because of the instability wreaked by Donald Trump’s war with Iran, Keir Starmer has said, adding that the moment called for a more ambitious deal with Brussels to strengthen trade and defence. His comments came as the US president again said he was considering pulling the US out of Nato , which he describe

stories.title: Man accused of plotting WA terror attack believed assault he was planning would be worse than Bondi beach shootings, court hears
stories.summary: Defence lawyer, former federal attorney general Christian Porter, says accused Jayson Joseph Michaels was ‘pretender full of big talk’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A man accused of plotting a mass casualty terror attack targeting public buildings and places of worship believed his assault would be worse than the Bondi beach mass shootings, a court has heard. Jayson Joseph Michaels detailed his alleged plan for

stories.title: ‘No evidence it’s real’: Officials see few signs Trump’s NATO rhetoric is turning into action
stories.summary: The Iran war is threatening to unravel the NATO alliance — again. But for all of President Donald Trump’s threats and anger over allies’ refusal to assist with the U.S. military campaign, his rhetoric has yet to be matched by any signs of concrete action. NATO diplomats, congressional aides and defense officials say the administration has not had the kinds of discussions needed to leave the nearly eight decade alliance. The U.S. has not started any debate inside NATO or issued specific directive

stories.title: ‘No plan, no legal basis’: Merz sparks backlash over desire to send Syrians home
stories.summary: BERLIN — German Chancellor Friedrich Merz is facing criticism from his coalition partners after declaring that 80 percent of Syrian refugees living in Germany should leave by the end of his term in 2029. Although it’s not the first time Merz has urged Syrians to return home to help rebuild their country following its long and brutal civil war that ended in 2024, his latest remarks have drawn a wider rebuke due to their specificity and the setting in which they were made — alongside Syrian

stories.title: Feuding French Socialists leave presidential prospects in disarray
stories.summary: PARIS — Deep fissures in the French Socialist Party that hamstrung the movement during recent municipal elections now threaten to derail its hopes of winning the presidency in 2027. The once-powerful faction is grappling with an identity crisis similar to that gripping left-of-center parties across Europe . But the warring blocs in Paris can’t even agree on the process of choosing a presidential candidate for next year, let alone who should run in a contest that, for now, the far right is tipped

stories.title: Britain’s EU point man says reset deal can be done by summer
stories.summary: LONDON — A deal to re-align key parts of the U.K. economy with the European Union is on course to be reached by the early summer, according to the minister in charge of negotiations with Europe, Nick Thomas-Symonds. Britain’s Minister for European Relations told POLITICO’s Westminster Insider podcast that, despite fraught talks over tuition fees for European students and the limits of a youth mobility scheme, he has made “very significant progress” with his EU counterpart, Maroš Šefčovič. Thomas

stories.title: Swedish PM offers deal that could see far-right allowed into government
stories.summary: Party, which has neo-Nazi roots, will hold ‘important ministerial posts within immigration’ if four-party coalition wins in September The Swedish prime minister, Ulf Kristersson, has said that he will allow the far-right Sweden Democrats (SD) into government for the first time – and give its members key ministerial posts – if his coalition wins the next general election. Despite becoming Sweden’s second biggest political party after the Social Democrats in the last election, SD currently plays o

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump says US close to ‘finishing the job’ in Iran during prime-time address
stories.summary: President claims that Iran was ‘right at the doorstep’ of gaining a nuclear weapon as he faces falling poll numbers and global energy crisis Trump says he is ‘absolutely’ considering withdrawing US from Nato Israel hits Iran with waves of attacks and says it killed top Hezbollah commander Trump has claimed that Iran was “right at the doorstep” of gaining a nuclear weapon . Earlier on Wednesday the president said he did not care about Iran’s stock of highly enriched uranium (HEU), arguing it was

stories.title: Trump to declare Iran war is winding down and others need to resolve Hormuz
stories.summary: President Donald Trump will use a primetime Oval Office address Wednesday night to declare that the month-long war in Iran is winding down, against a backdrop of  spiking oil prices  and  increasingly dismal poll numbers . The president has telegraphed that message in interviews, social media posts and public comments over the past 24 hours, laying the groundwork for a speech that is expected to claim that all military objectives have been met, according to six people familiar wit

stories.title: Trump warns U.S. will hit Iran ‘extremely hard’ over next two to three weeks
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Wednesday said the U.S. is getting “very close” to finishing its objectives in Iran but warned that the next two weeks will involve intense military activity. “We are on track to complete all of America’s military objectives shortly, very shortly,” the president said in his first primetime address since the war in Iran began. “We’re going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks.” The president also called on countries who get their oil through the St

stories.title: Cambodia: Alleged cyber scam boss extradited to China
stories.summary: Authorities say Li Xiong was arrested in Cambodia and deported at the request of Chinese authorities. He is linked to an accused scam center kingpin who was extradited in January.

stories.title: Three Reichsbürger backers face trial in Germany over coup and kidnapping plot
stories.summary: Three alleged backers of Germany's Reichsbürger "United Patriots" group face trial in Munich over supporting coup plans and a plot to kidnap ex-health minister.

stories.title: Families condemn UK ‘impotence’ over UAE ‘social media misuse’ detentions
stories.summary: Ministers accused of being too fearful of offending Emirates to help Britons detained for sharing images of war Middle East crisis live – latest updates The families of UK citizens held in the United Arab Emirates over allegations that they shared images of the conflict with Iran have voiced frustration at the British government’s failure to help. Several British citizens are among more than 100 foreign nationals who have been detained under draconian Emirate rules that outlaw publishing or shar

stories.title: Trump lashes out at Nato: will Europe stand up to him? - The Latest
stories.summary: Donald Trump has said he is considering pulling the US out of Nato, likening the alliance to a ‘paper tiger’. It comes after weeks of denouncements from the US president against allies for not helping to reopen the strait of Hormuz. When asked about Trump’s comments, Keir Starmer said: ‘Nato is the single most effective military alliance the world has ever seen’ and ‘whatever the noise, I’m going to act in the British national interest in all the decisions that I make’. Lucy Hough speaks to the

stories.title: High times or low blows? Experts fail to clear air over German drug legalisation
stories.summary: Cannabis policy still divisive two years in, with SPD hailing it while CDU minister says it is risk to young people’s health It was a landmark piece of legislation passed by Germany’s previous, centre-left-led government: a measure that legalised the personal recreational use of cannabis for over-18s despite warnings from critics it would cause a steep rise in the drug’s use, including by teenagers, and boost criminal gangs. Two years on, controversy over the move has still not been stubbed out,

stories.title: ‘System malfunction’ causes robotaxis to stall in the middle of the road in China
stories.summary: Distressed riders who were stranded for hours say Apollo Go customer service agents offered ‘useless platitudes’ A “system malfunction” has caused several self-driving robotaxis to stall in the middle of the road in China, police have confirmed, after distressed riders were stranded for hours. Local authorities in the central Chinese city of Wuhan said they began receiving calls “one after another” on Tuesday night from riders reporting that autonomous vehicles operated by the Chinese internet c

stories.title: CPS advising UK police on investigation into Andrew and Mandelson links to Epstein
stories.summary: Crown Prosecution Service confirms support on inquiries after arrests on suspicion of misconduct in public office Police are receiving advice from prosecutors as part of their inquiries into Peter Mandelson and Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s links to Jeffrey Epstein. The former duke of York and the former UK ambassador to the US were both arrested in February on suspicion of misconduct in public office over their connections with the late financier. They have since been released under investigatio

stories.title: Israel hits Iran with waves of attacks and says it killed top Hezbollah commander
stories.summary: War shows little sign of easing despite Donald Trump claiming Iranian leadership ‘just asked’ for ceasefire Middle East crisis – live updates Israel unleashed two waves of attacks on Tehran and said it had killed a senior Hezbollah commander on Wednesday with little sign of the war easing up despite Donald Trump repeating a claim that Iran’s leadership was seeking a ceasefire. The US president, writing on social media, said that Iran’s president had “just asked” for a ceasefire and that American

stories.title: Resident doctors accuse Keir Starmer of sabotaging talks to end pay and jobs dispute
stories.summary: British Medical Association leaders say PM’s threat to cut 1,000 new roles makes next week’s strike action more likely Resident doctors have accused Keir Starmer of damaging the prospects of a deal to end their pay and jobs dispute by threatening to cut 1,000 new jobs for medics in the NHS. The claim from the British Medical Association leaders came just before the Thursday deadline given by the prime minister for the union to accept the government’s final offer. Continue reading...

stories.title: Britain to host 35 countries for strait of Hormuz talks, says Starmer
stories.summary: US understood not to be invited directly to talks that will explore ways of reopening critical waterway Middle East crisis – live updates The UK will convene 35 countries – excluding the US – to explore ways to reopen the strait of Hormuz, the vital shipping route for oil and gas that has been blocked by Iran. Keir Starmer, the prime minister, said the next phase of discussions in the joint British and French efforts to secure the waterway would be held on Thursday, with Yvette Cooper, the forei

stories.title: Top Iranian official injured in strike on Tehran – as it happened
stories.summary: This liveblog is closed, please follow our new liveblog here Houthi forces in Yemen have claimed responsibility for a missile attack on southern Israel this morning, saying it was a joint operation with Iran and Hezbollah. In a statement, the Houthi movement said it carried out its third missile attack in the conflict “in conjunction with Iran and Hezbollah in Lebanon”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Top Senate Democrat says ‘House Republicans caved’ as deal to fund DHS moves forward – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Republican leaders agree to advance funding deal to end DHS shutdown Sign up for the Breaking News US email We’re starting to get pictures from outside the US supreme court ahead of oral arguments in Trump v Barbara, which will decide if the administration’s attempts to restrict birthright citizenship are unconstitutional. Donald Trump has just arrived, and plans to listen to arguments at the court – the first time a sitting president has attended arguments. Co

stories.title: Track Australia’s fuel prices, service station outages and shipments in charts
stories.summary: Check how much petrol prices have risen since the US and Israel’s war on Iran began in late February Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Hundreds of service stations across Australia have run empty, fuel prices are elevated and oil shipments have been cancelled. Australia is battling a fuel crises as Iran’s closure of the strait of Hormuz continues to bite. The federal government has released fuel reserves, cut fuel excise taxes and rolled out a national fuel security pl

stories.title: Oil price falls and markets rally after Trump says Iran war over in ‘two to three weeks’
stories.summary: US president’s claim that conflict is nearing end prompts 15% drop in Brent crude and stock market climb in Asia Business live – latest updates Oil prices tumbled and stock markets have rallied across the world after Donald Trump said the war in Iran would end in “two to three weeks”. Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil, fell as low as $98.35 a barrel on Wednesday, down more than 15% on the previous day and its lowest level in a week. It later recovered some ground, ending the day a

stories.title: US Supreme Court debates citizenship, with rare Trump visit
stories.summary: Donald Trump is the first president to appear at a Supreme Court oral argument, as he pushes to end birthright citizenship in the US.

stories.title: South African army arrive in crime hotspots to help tackle gangs
stories.summary: The army has been deployed to five of South Africa's nine provinces for a year-long operation.

stories.title: Trump isn't immune from civil claims his 6 January speech incited riot, judge says
stories.summary: Trump spoke to a crowd of his supporters at the rally before the mob’s attack disrupted the joint session of Congress for certifying Democrat Joe Biden’s 2020 electoral victory over Trump.

stories.title: Families cram into Greek court for trial into deadliest train crash
stories.summary: Fifty-seven people were killed when two trains collided in 2023 in the worst rail accident in Greek history, with 36 accused in a trial expected to last years.

stories.title: What do Trump's latest comments on leaving Nato mean for the alliance?
stories.summary: Trump has criticised Nato members for showing a lack of support for US objectives in Iran.

stories.title: Switzerland eyes dropping purchase of US Patriot air defence system over delivery delays
stories.summary: Switzerland, which is not in NATO, had ordered five Patriot systems in 2022, with delivery scheduled to begin this year and to be completed in 2028.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill seven in Beirut as it vows to occupy southern Lebanon after war ends
stories.summary: Heavy strikes hit Lebanon's capital Beirut overnight as Israel claimed it had struck a "senior Hezbollah commander" and another member of the group. Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said his country's military intends to occupy a swathe of southern Lebanon.

stories.title: Police disperse Jerusalem protest over Israel’s death penalty law
stories.summary: Israeli police broke up a protest outside parliament in Jerusalem as demonstrators voiced anger over a new law allowing the execution of Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks.

stories.title: Iran strikes tanker off Qatar coast as Tehran's attacks on Gulf states persist
stories.summary: Qatar said it had intercepted two Iranian cruise missiles, while a third struck a fuel tanker off its coast. All 21 crew members were safely evacuated, with no injuries or environmental damage reported.

stories.title: Reeves criticises Trump for starting Iran war with no ‘clear plan’ to get out of it – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Starmer says he understands why people are concerned about the cost of living. He says he has already set out a five-point plan to deal with the crisis. Just look at what’s happening today. Today your energy bills will be cut because of the action that we took at the budget. And whatever happens in Iran, that price is now fixed until July. The most effective way we can support the cost of living in Britain is to push for de-escalation in the Middle East, and a reopening o

stories.title: Two dead and one missing after trying to cross Channel to UK
stories.summary: First fatal incident this year occurred hours after £16.2m ‘stop the boats’ deal agreed between Britain and France Europe live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates Two people have died and another is missing after trying to cross the Channel from France to the UK on Wednesday morning. It is the first fatal incident in the Channel this year. The deaths occurred just hours after an interim £16.2m “stop the boats” deal was agreed between the UK and France which will be in place until

stories.title: UK is most vulnerable European country to jet fuel shortages, Ryanair boss says
stories.summary: Michael O’Leary says UK’s reliance on Kuwait for jet fuel supply amid Iran war exposes it to possible shortages The UK is the most vulnerable country in Europe to potential jet fuel shortages as the Iran war throttles supplies from the Gulf, the boss of Ryanair has said. Michael O’Leary, the chief executive of the budget airline, said Britain would be the most exposed to jet fuel shortages because it relies on Kuwait for about 25% of its supply. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK food inflation ‘could hit 9%’, trade body warns as Reeves meets retail chiefs
stories.summary: Discussion on how to ease impact from Iran war coincides with Food and Drink Federation almost tripling forecast Food inflation could hit 9% in the UK this year even if the strait of Hormuz opens within the next few weeks, figures suggest, as the Iran war pushes up energy prices. The Food and Drink Federation (FDF), which represents 12,000 food and drink manufacturers, has predicted prices will rise by “at least” 9% by the end of 2026, almost tripling a forecast of 3.2% that was made before the

stories.title: Nasa’s Artemis II rocket lifts off for historic moon mission
stories.summary: Mass of spectators cheers dazzling Florida launch as astronauts head to moon for first time in almost 54 years Nasa’s moon rocket Artemis II launched on Wednesday evening, carrying astronauts to the moon for the first time in almost 54 years. The rocket is now orbiting Earth and will continue to do so until Thursday, when the translunar injection burn will take place and send it on the rest of its 240,000-mile journey to the moon. Inside the Orion capsule, the four astronauts onboard immediately

stories.title: Penny Wong to join talks with 35 countries, excluding US, to explore ways to reopen strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Talks, convened by the UK, will examine ‘all viable diplomatic and political measures’ to get critical waterway open Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia will join talks with 35 nations, convened by the United Kingdom, to explore ways to reopen the strait of Hormuz, the government confirmed on Thursday. The UK prime minister, Keir Starmer, announced the meeting on Wednesday, which will exclude the United Sta

stories.title: Artemis II: Nasa’s crewed rocket lifts off to begin 10-day lunar journey – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Full report – Nasa’s Artemis II rocket lifts off for historic moon mission There’s potentially alarming news from AccuWeather about a solar flare, which the forecasting service says could affect the Artemis mission. While not an official Nasa source for weather and climate information or predictions, AccuWeather has been monitoring launch day conditions, and is reporting them on its own blog. An X1.5 solar flare that occurred early on March 30 produced an Earth-dire

stories.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes in Indonesia, sparking tsunami alert
stories.summary: The quake had depth of 35km and its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate in Northern Molucca Sea region A magnitude 7.4 earthquake has struck the Northern Molucca Sea region in Indonesia, the United States Geological Survey (USGS) said. The quake, which hit early on Thursday local time, had depth of 35km and its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-northwest of Ternate, Indonesia, the USGS said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Death of Rohingya refugee left in parking lot by US border agents ruled a homicide
stories.summary: Nurul Amin Shah, 56, who was visually impaired, was left outside Buffalo Tim Hortons on cold night and later died Authorities have ruled that the death of Nurul Amin Shah, a 56-year-old Rohingya refugee from Myanmar who was left by immigration agents at a restaurant in Buffalo, was a homicide . Shah, who was visually impaired, died on 24 February, five days after US Border Patrol agents dropped him off in the parking lot of a Tim Hortons on a cold winter night without notifying his family or att

stories.title: Alleged Bondi terrorist Naveed Akram denied suppression order over identities of family members
stories.summary: Lawyers for accused had argued names of family members should be suppressed due to fears for their mental and physical safety Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The alleged Bondi attacker has been denied a suppression order over his family member’s names and home and work addresses after a collective of media organisations won a challenge against the bid. In the Downing Centre local court on Thursday, judge Hugh Don

stories.title: Bob’s Burgers actor Eugene Mirman rescued from fiery car crash by New Hampshire governor’s detail
stories.summary: Actor and comedian known for voicing Gene Belcher, as well as roles in Flight of the Conchords and Archer, has suffered serious injuries Bob’s Burgers voice actor and comedian Eugene Mirman has suffered serious injuries after crashing his car into a toll plaza in New Hampshire, before he was pulled from the fiery wreckage by a state trooper assigned to protect the state’s governor. The crash happened just before noon Tuesday when a northbound electric vehicle struck the Bedford toll plaza and ca

stories.title: New Zealand signs defence pact with Cook Islands after quarrel over China deal
stories.summary: Agreement comes after Wellington halted millions in aid to its former colony after Cook Islands formed strategic partnership with Beijing New Zealand and the Cook Islands have signed a defence and security declaration, ending a year-long diplomatic row that erupted after the Cook Islands struck strategic agreements with China. The Cook Islands was a dependent New Zealand colony from 1901-65 but has since operated as a self-governing nation in “free association” with New Zealand. Its roughly 17,0

stories.title: Asia ramps up use of dirty fuels to cover energy shortfall triggered by Iran war
stories.summary: South Korea will delay the shutdown of coal-fired plants, while the Philippines also plans to boost the output of its coal-burning plants Governments across Asia are ramping up their use of coal, the dirtiest fossil fuel, as they try to cover huge energy shortfalls triggered by the US-Israel war on Iran. The move has triggered warnings from climate experts who point to coal’s devastating environmental impact, and say the energy crisis should be a wake up call for governments to invest in renewab

stories.title: Germany: Hope fades for stranded humpback whale's survival
stories.summary: Local authorities have announced that they will cease efforts to guide a young humpback whale to its home waters. Now a restricted area has been established to allow it to "die in peace."

stories.title: NASA's Artemis II rocket lifts off for the moon
stories.summary: The four astronauts aboard the Artemis II are headed toward a historic journey around the moon. The test flight marks a major step in NASA's ambitious program of establishing a long-term presence on the lunar surface.

stories.title: Trump says US will 'finish the job' in Iran
stories.summary: President Trump said the US' "core strategic objectives" in Iran war are "nearing completion." He also urged other countries to show "courage" and "take care" of the Strait of Hormuz. DW has more.

stories.title: Powerful earthquake strikes Molucca ⁠Sea near Indonesia
stories.summary: A major 7.4-magnitude earthquake has jolted eastern Indonesia, triggering fears of a tsunami. But the initial warning has now been lifted.

stories.title: Russian military plane crash in Crimea kills at least 29
stories.summary: The plane crashed in a mountainous forest area not far from Crimea's capital, Simferopol. Since the Kremlin sent troops into Ukraine, incidents involving Russian military planes have occurred frequently.

stories.title: General strike empties streets in Ramallah over Israel’s death penalty law
stories.summary: Shops shut and streets fell quiet in Ramallah as a general strike was held in protest against Israel’s newly approved death penalty law targeting Palestinians.

stories.title: DR Congo declares national holiday after reaching World Cup for first time in 52 years
stories.summary: This is just the second time that DR Congo have reached the World Cup finals, sparking celebrations in Kinshasa.

stories.title: Shops closed across West Bank in general strike against Israel's death penalty law for Palestinians
stories.summary: Thousands marched to protest the passing of the law, which critics have slammed as unjust and demanded it immediately been scrapped.

stories.title: Paris catacombs to reopen in 2026 after major safety renovation
stories.summary: The Paris Catacombs will reopen in spring 2026 after major structural and visual upgrades aimed at protecting visitors and centuries-old human remains.

stories.title: Teams halt efforts for whale stranded off German coast as chances of rescue 'very low'
stories.summary: Since it was spotted last week the whale had prompted a large-scale rescue operation involving firefighters, scientists and the maritime police.

stories.title: Explosions at Burundi ammunition depot kill civilians, witnesses say
stories.summary: Powerful blasts destroyed homes and sparked panic in the city of Bujumbura on Tuesday night.

stories.title: Fuel shortages spark long queues at petrol stations in Johannesburg
stories.summary: Motorists lined up at petrol stations in Johannesburg as fuel shortages and supply limits caused disruptions and raised concerns over rising costs.

stories.title: Trump to give primetime address on war as questions swirl over his next move
stories.summary: The US president finds himself under growing pressure at home to avoid a protracted conflict.

stories.title: US Supreme Court casts doubt on Donald Trump's bid to limit birthright citizenship
stories.summary: Trump signed an executive order on his return to the White House last year decreeing that children born to parents in the United States illegally or on temporary visas would not automatically become US citizens

stories.title: Iran launches biggest salvo of missiles in three weeks at Israel, military officials say
stories.summary: Iranian missile fire on Israel has slowed in the past two weeks to around 10-15 missiles a day, down from around 90 on the first day of the war.

stories.title: Megan Thee Stallion taken to hospital during Moulin Rouge performance
stories.summary: The rapper says she felt faint while performing in the Broadway show and was later diagnosed with "extreme exhaustion".

stories.title: Trump considers pulling US out of 'paper tiger' NATO
stories.summary: Meanwhile, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the UK would host a meeting of about 35 countries to discuss reopening the Strait of Hormuz, which has been crippled by the Iran war.

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime
stories.summary: Will German citizens feel safer if more immigrants are deported? Chancellor Friedrich Merz has been giving that impression. But the reality is more complex.

stories.title: Emergency services respond to fire in central Israel after Iranian missile attack
stories.summary: Emergency teams responded to a fire in central Israel after Iranian missile strikes, with several people reported injured.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.summary: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Asia's migrant workers debate if Gulf jobs are worth deadly risk of Iran war
stories.summary: Iran's strikes on Gulf states have been especially hard for migrants who have long supported these economies to lift their families back home from poverty.

stories.title: Molly the border collie rescued after a week waiting for injured owner in New Zealand’s remote backcountry
stories.summary: A rescue mission involving volunteer helicopter crew and public donations ended in joy after Molly was located and brought home A spot of furry black and white appears among the jagged rocks of New Zealand’s alpine backcountry. It is Molly the border collie, sitting near the foot of a waterfall where she had been separated from her owner one week earlier. Molly was rescued on Tuesday after an avalanche of donations from the public funded a volunteer team made up of former helicopter pilots and c

stories.title: Finnish up! Claire Aho’s colour revolution – in pictures
stories.summary: The pioneering Nordic artist brought wit, verve and cinematic flair to postwar photography. A new exhibition celebrates her vibrant visual style Continue reading...

stories.title: In Europe, lobbyists are using soaring fuel prices to make the case for more dirty energy
stories.summary: The argument for transitioning to renewables seems stronger than ever – and yet, attacks mount on the carbon price scheme that underpins the EU’s success at cutting pollution • Don’t get This Is Europe delivered to your inbox? Sign up here On the one hand, experts say, Europe is better prepared for this energy crisis than the last. On the other, it is still waging a culture war against the most obvious path out. Fuel prices have soared to ruinous levels since the Iran war left ships of oil and l

stories.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
stories.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.

stories.title: India news: Jet fuel prices reach record high amid Iran war
stories.summary: The Indian government has stepped in to limit the impact of record-high jet fuel prices on airlines and air fares. Commercial LPG cylinders have also gotten more expensive.

stories.title: Germany news: New fuel price law prompts huge midday spike
stories.summary: Filling stations responded to the new limit of one daily price hike with a hefty 7.5 cent average increase. Meanwhile, the BND spy agency confused workers by announcing an extra holiday on April Fool's day. DW has more.

stories.title: Migrant boat capsizes in Turkey leaving 18 dead while 21 were rescued
stories.summary: Turkish authorities say the vessel attempted to evade its Coast Guard before sinking. At least 18 people drowned in the incident, 21 were rescued and an unknown number were missing.

stories.title: Germany's chancellor said 80% of Syrians should return to their home country in the next three years. Syrians in Germany are outraged.
stories.summary: According to Chancellor Friedrich Merz, 80% of Syrians living in Germany are to return to their home country within the next three years. The chancellor's remark has drawn fierce criticism.

stories.title: Iran war: Strikes on steel deal painful blow to economy
stories.summary: The US-Israeli strikes on Iran's two biggest steel mills will likely harm Tehran's ability to wage war, but could also cripple Iran's economy in irreversible ways.

stories.title: Undiagnosed TB pose challenge for South Africa, Mozambique
stories.summary: Africa is making progress in reducing tuberculosis (TB). But experts say missed cases and drug-resistant infections are complicating the fight, particularly in South Africa and Mozambique.

stories.title: Is Pakistan setting up a buffer zone in Afghanistan?
stories.summary: Tensions on the Afghanistan-Pakistan border are escalating amid claims of a Pakistani "security zone" and civilian casualties. Will calls for a ceasefire have any affect?

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives call cannabis legalization 'a nonstarter' as 2-year report is filed
stories.summary: Conservatives see a two-year report on Germany's partial legalization of cannabis as evidence that it's been a failure. While the findings may be more nuanced, researchers did identify several problems.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq qualify but Iran doubts loom
stories.summary: Iraq have overcome tough circumstances to reach the World Cup for the first time in 30 years, with the lineup now complete. But Iran's potential withdrawal still hangs over a tournament fraught with political issues.

stories.title: 'My six-year-old has nosebleeds': Chiang Mai air pollution sparks health fears
stories.summary: It's peak fire season in the Thai city, which now ranks as one of the world's most polluted.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war could end in 'two weeks', with or without deal
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said the war in Iran could end in 'two weeks, maybe three', adding that 'whether we have a deal or not, it's irrelevant.' The White House announced Trump will address the nation with an update on Wednesday night.

stories.title: Divorced couples in Japan can now share custody of their children
stories.summary: Japan was, until recently, the only G7 country to not recognise the legal concept of joint custody.

stories.title: Lorry drivers protest near Paris over soaring fuel prices
stories.summary: Dozens of lorries and tractors staged a slow-moving protest east of Paris to draw attention to rising fuel costs linked to the Middle East war.

stories.title: 'It's phenomenal!': Excitement in Florida ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: BBC’s Leyla Khodabakhshi speaks to locals and visitors in Florida ahead of the Artemis II mission blast off.

stories.title: Thousands lose their jobs in deep cuts at tech giant Oracle
stories.summary: It is thought that thousands of people may have lost their jobs at Oracle, one of the world's largest tech companies.

stories.title: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
stories.summary: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

stories.title: EU prosecutors seek to lift immunity of 11 Greek MPs in farm subsidy scam
stories.summary: European prosecutors have urged Greece to lift immunity for 11 MPs over a multimillion-euro farm subsidy fraud, piling pressure on PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis.

stories.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
stories.summary: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

stories.title: Traditional Easter ‘monas’ get a contemporary makeover in Barcelona
stories.summary: A chocolatier in Barcelona is reimagining traditional Catalan Easter cakes, known as “monas,” with bold colours, modern designs and less sugary flavours.

stories.title: Iran war economic shocks will last 'months', says Australia's PM
stories.summary: Anthony Albanese used a rare TV address to tell Australians that the "months ahead may not be easy" amid the global effects of the war.

stories.title: Brazil judge blocks Sugarloaf Mountain zipline
stories.summary: The project to build a zipline linking the popular Rio tourist spot to Morro da Urca proved controversial.

stories.title: Mass robotaxi malfunction halts traffic in Chinese city
stories.summary: Baidu has not responded to a request for comment about the outage, which affected at least 100 cars.

stories.title: 'One leg in uniform, one in a suit': How one young German is helping reshape Europe's security
stories.summary: Léocadie Reimers represents a generation rethinking security, combining her experiences from military service with her work in private sector defence.

stories.title: Devastated Italians reckon with 'third apocalypse' of World Cup failure
stories.summary: Azzurri fans and management alike are pondering what went wrong after Italy fail to qualify for third World Cup in a row.

stories.title: Securing Iran's highly-enriched uranium stockpiles could prove risky, experts say
stories.summary: Experts, analysts and former US government officials say Washington's alleged plans to deploy ground units to Iran to retrieve uranium stockpiles could expose personnel to potential attacks, radiation and chemical risks.

stories.title: Surveillance video shows kidnapping of US journalist in Baghdad
stories.summary: Surveillance footage appears to show the abduction of US journalist Shelly Kittleson in Baghdad, as officials say she remains missing and an investigation into the kidnapping is ongoing.

stories.title: In California primary, an electoral disaster is looming for Democrats
stories.summary: Two months before a crucial primary in one of the most liberal states in the US, Democrats are facing a self-made political catastrophe. Too many candidates for governor are fragmenting the party's electorate and could make a Republican the successor of Gavin Newsom.

stories.title: Visszalép a DK-s jelölt Nagy Ervin körzetében
stories.summary: Szabó Zsolt azt írta: a Tisza jelöltjét támogatja, és a szavazóit is erre buzdította. Korábban több DK-s jelölt lépett már vissza ellenzéki pártok javára.

stories.title: Nem kell megszelídíteni, hiszen épp az a jó benne, hogy sírunk tőle
stories.summary: Az üveges tormák elveszítik az erejüket, illatukat, ízüket, de sokan félnek a friss tormától, pedig az ennek az ízesítőnek a legigazibb, legjobb formája. Miért érdemes inkább frissen megvenni, és mit kell kezdeni vele?

stories.title: Képtelen lebontatni a kormányhivatal a szabálytalanul, régészeti lelőhelyre épült óbudai teniszklubot
stories.summary: Sőt, a legutóbbi hatósági ellenőrzésen azt állapították meg, hogy tovább bővítették.

stories.title: Világszerte egyre inkább ösztönzik a spórolást, nálunk az árstop az ellenkezőjét üzeni
stories.summary: Az iráni háború hatásai miatt otthoni munkavégzést, és az autózás, illetve a repülés visszafogását kéri már az Európai Bizottság is.

stories.title: Az EU betiltotta az MI-generált tartalmakat az intézményeinek hivatalos kommunikációjában
stories.summary: A továbbiakban az Európai Bizottság, a Tanács és a Parlament munkatársai sem használhatják így a mesterséges intelligenciát.

stories.title: Hajdú Péter szerint Szabó Bence állítása bizonyítja, hogy a rendőrség és a NAV is jól végzi a dolgát a Fidesz-kormány alatt
stories.summary: A műsorvezető arra reagált, hogy Szabó Bence a Telex műsorában elmondta, még a NAV nyomozójaként ő is dolgozott Hajdú adócsalási ügyén.

stories.title: Menczer nem hisz a kormányközeli Nézőpontnak, de azt elismerte, még 7-8 ezer szavazóra van szüksége
stories.summary: Tizenegy nap alatt kell meggyőznie őket.

stories.title: Hivatali visszaélés miatt letartóztattak két magánnyomozót és egy rendőrt
stories.summary: A gyanú szerint a rendőr pénzért cserébe osztott meg nem nyilvános adatokat a két magánnyomozóval.

stories.title: A Hintalovon Alapítványnak is jut a Szabó Bencének összegyűjtött adományokból
stories.summary: A szervezet a felajánlott összeget a gyerekekkel szembeni szexuális visszaélésekkel kapcsolatos munkájára fordítja.

stories.title: A zabkásafőzés világsztárjai semmi pénzért nem használnának fakanalat
stories.summary: Skót duda, éneklő zabszemek és hömpölygő, heves folyók. Az Arany Spatula olyan, mintha Wes Anderson rendezett volna gasztronómiai dokumentumfilmet egy elszánt álmodozóról, a mókuskerékből való meglógásról és a közösség erejéről.

stories.title: Nem indult eljárás Buddha személyiségi jogainak megsértése miatt, mert a NAIH „le van terhelve”
stories.summary: Péterfalvi Attila, a hivatal elnöke a Hvg.hu-nak azt mondta, ha a Tisza Párthoz köthető informatikusnak nem fáj ez annyira, hogy sérelmezze, akkor ők sem indítanak hivatalból eljárást.

stories.title: Egy gyilkosságért elítélt amerikai a 44 éve halott kutyájára hivatkozva bizonyítaná be, hogy ártatlan
stories.summary: Paul Kovacich felesége rejtélyes körülmények között tűnt el a nyolcvanas években. Később megtalálták a maradványait, és a férjét vádolták meg a gyilkossággal. Az egykori rendőr perében az elpusztult kutyája is szerepet játszott, ám most úgy tűnik, hogy eljárásbeli hibát követhettek el vele az ügyészek.

stories.title: Elindult a misszió, amivel az emberiség messzebbre jut az űrben, mint valaha
stories.summary: Az Artemis-2-vel négy űrhajós fogja megkerülni a Holdat, tíz napot fognak a világűrben tölteni, és messzebbre jutnak, mint ember valaha. Az expedíció döcögősen indult, többször is elhalasztották, de végül kisebb hibák elhárítása után fellőtték az Orion űrkapszulát szállító SLS rakétát.

stories.title: Serbian local elections: Is Vucic's party losing its grip?
stories.summary: Serbia's ruling SNS once again secured victories in local elections — but sometimes just by a few hundred votes. Narrowing margins suggest a shifting political landscape and growing momentum for anti-government protests.

stories.title: US judge blocks Trump order that barred funding for NPR, PBS
stories.summary: A federal judge has blocked a presidential directive that barred funding for NPR and PBS media entities. Separately, an appeals court panel paused a federal judge's order to bring VOA employees back to work.

stories.title: Lebanese justice minister calls for Israel negotiations to avert humanitarian catastrophe
stories.summary: Lebanese justice minister warns of 'dramatic' situation in the country as Israeli floats extended buffer zone on its southern border. Nassar calls for diplomacy to avert a catastrophe as he lashes at Hezbolahh for sabotaging diplomatic efforts by not laying down weapons.

stories.title: Moment US strike sends massive fireball into sky over Iran’s Isfahan
stories.summary: Large explosions and fires were seen over Isfahan, a central Iranian city hosting military and nuclear facilities.

stories.title: Israeli army clashes with Palestinians during north Jerusalem raid
stories.summary: Israeli army clashes with Palestinians during an overnight raid in Kafr Aqab north of Jerusalem

stories.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba after US allows passage despite energy blockade
stories.summary: Cuba used to receive most of its oil from Venezuela, but those shipments were halted ever since the US attacked the South American country and arrested its leader Nicolás Maduro in early January.

stories.title: Indonesia arrests Scottish man sought by Spain in connection with international crime syndicate
stories.summary: Police in Scotland and Spain carried out raids in connection with Lyons' case last week resulting in multiple arrests with the help of Europol, the European Union law enforcement cooperation hub.

stories.title: Spheres of influence: the Bauhaus’s radical female photographers – in pictures
stories.summary: The images are famous, but the women who took them are often forgotten. An inspiring exhibition focuses on the pioneering ‘new vision’ of Marianne Brandt, Lucia Moholy and more Continue reading...

stories.title: Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician and social activist, dies aged 88
stories.summary: Lewis’s son Avi Lewis was elected leader of progressive New Democratic party a day before his father’s death Stephen Lewis, the Canadian diplomat, politician and human rights advocate, who spent decades tirelessly working to focus global attention on the HIV/Aids epidemic, has died of cancer. Lewis, who served as the Canadian ambassador to the United Nations, as well as the head of Ontario’s New Democratic party (NDP), was 88. Continue reading...

stories.title: Kvóták, postások, buszok – az RMDSZ mindent megtesz, hogy szállítson két mandátumot a Fidesznek
stories.summary: Miközben az RMDSZ hivatalosan csak „segít” a levélszavazatok célba juttatásában, a színfalak mögött kőkemény mozgósítási gépezet zakatol. Forrásaink szerint megyékre lebontott számbeli elvárások, a postásoktól „elkerekített” listák és szervezett buszos utazások szegélyezik az utat a konzulátusokig. Tánczos Barna miniszterelnök-helyettes szerint a módszerek egy része „ősrégi” és „nem egészséges”, Antal Árpád stratégiai igazgató pedig mély hallgatásba burkolózik a kvóták kapcsán. Mi áll a feltétel

stories.title: Trump erősen fontolgatja, hogy kiléptesse az Egyesült Államokat a NATO-ból
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök erről azután beszélt, hogy külügyminisztere közölte, fel kell tenni a kérdést, hogy „miért is vagyunk a NATO-ban”.

stories.title: A Szondi utca alkotója poénból újragondolná a Nerdőt
stories.summary: Mi is elhittük, hogy Roznár Levente beszáll Puzsérék sorozatába, de az egész csak áprilisi poén volt.

stories.title: A Breaking Badből ismert szerrel mérgeztek meg egy olasz nőt és 15 éves lányát
stories.summary: Eddig azt hitték, hogy ételmérgezés miatt haltak meg karácsonykor, de ricinnel ölték meg őket.

stories.title: Milka csokikat hívott vissza a fogyasztóvédelem
stories.summary: A noisette és a törtmogyorós termékeket érinti az intézkedés.

stories.title: Újabb Győr környéki vonalakat vesz át a GYSEV a MÁV-tól
stories.summary: Április elsejétől Csorna és Pápa közt kényelmesebb lesz az utazás.

stories.title: Ukrajna húsvéti tűzszünetet ajánlott, Oroszország nem kért belőle
stories.summary: Moszkva „ukrán PR-fogásnak” nevezte a javaslatot.

stories.title: Áthelyezték Szabó Bence ügyét a fővárosi ügyészségre
stories.summary: Szabó Bence a Telex élő műsorában azt mondta, tapasztalatai szerint ez azt jelenti, hogy kevésbé fontos most az ő ügye, ezért döntött így a Központi Nyomozó Főügyészség.

stories.title: Összetört a Trump tömeges deportálásának arcává vált Kristi Noem a férjéről készült fényképek miatt
stories.summary: A Trump tömeges deportálását levezénylő miniszter férjéről kiderült, hogy hatalmas műmelleket hordva beszélgetett fétismodellekkel.

stories.title: Válságtanácskozás lesz az olasz fociban, miután sorrendben harmadszor hiányoznak a foci-vb-ről
stories.summary: A válság egyértelmű, a török kispadon viszont olasz szakember, Vincenzo Montella ül.

stories.title: Szabó Bence: Remélem, Hajdú Péter úr nosztalgiával gondol majd rám, én nyomoztam ki a csalási ügyét
stories.summary: Hajdú 2020-ban kapott felfüggesztett börtönt költségvetési csalás miatt.

stories.title: Csütörtökre rendkívüli közgyűlést hívott össze a győri polgármester a Fidesz kampányrendezvényén történtek miatt
stories.summary: Pintér Bence felkérdezné a rendőrséget, ehhez azonban közgyűlési határozatra van szükség.

stories.title: Kizárták a kiskunhalasi ifiket, miután 50 gólt kaptak egy bajnoki focimeccsen
stories.summary: Az U19-es csapat negyedszer állt ki létszámhiányosan.

stories.title: Bíróság elé kell állnia a győri gázolónak, akár öt év börtönt is kaphat
stories.summary: Az ügyészség friss közleményéből az is kiderült, hogy a férfi által vezetett Aston Martin majdnem a megengedett sebesség duplájánál, 93 km/h-nál sodródott ki.

stories.title: Kárpátalján találat ért egy alállomást, orosz drón először jutott ilyen messzire
stories.summary: Az áramhálózat alállomását érte orosz dróntalálat szerdán. A kár nem jelentős, de beszédes, hogy a fronttól ennél távolabbra eddig csak rakéta jutott Kárpátalján a háború négy éve alatt.

stories.title: Talán csak a Mi Hazánk lehet olyan keresztény, mint a Fidesz, üzeni a kormányközeli keresztény agytröszt
stories.summary: Keresztényeknek szánt vallási iránytűt készített egy kormányközeli alapítvány, amit az MTI közzé is tett. Arra jutottak, hogy a Fidesz a legkeresztényebb.

stories.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
stories.summary: The Iran war could end soon or drag on for much longer. Whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back.

stories.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
stories.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

stories.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
stories.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

stories.title: Who are the Artemis II astronauts heading to the Moon?
stories.summary: The crew, who are accomplished pilots, engineers and scientists, reveal their hopes, fears and family sacrifices.

stories.title: Shock, sadness and relief in town at centre of Australia's seven-month manhunt for Dezi Freeman
stories.summary: Porepunkah has been in the spotlight since Dezi Freeman shot dead two police officers last year.

stories.title: Attack on Michigan synagogue inspired by Iran-backed Hezbollah, FBI says
stories.summary: The FBI says the synagogue attack earlier in March was inspired by Hezbollah, targeting Michigan’s largest Jewish temple with a truck packed with fuel.

stories.title: Eiffel Tower lights up in Paris for Celine Dion's return to the stage
stories.summary: To celebrate Celine Dion's 58th birthday and announce her official return to the stage, messages in several languages are projected onto the Eiffel Tower.

stories.title: Trump tells allies to ‘go get your own oil’ amid Iranian attacks on Gulf
stories.summary: Trump's comments come on the same day that gas prices in the US jumped past an average of $4 for the first time since 2022 as fuel prices continue to spike worldwide.

stories.title: With Spielberg's help, a 101-year-old Auschwitz survivor becomes warrior against hate
stories.summary: People who watch the countless interviews Ginette Kolinka gives cannot say that they didn’t know about the extermination of 6 million European Jews by the Nazis and their collaborators.

stories.title: Around 30% of France's teachers strike in protest against job cuts and low wages
stories.summary: The strike action comes as anger grows among France's teachers about job cuts, class closures and stagnating wages.

stories.title: This German village relies on renewables to avoid rising energy costs
stories.summary: The German village of Feldheim is using wind, solar and biogas energy to shield itself from rising energy prices linked to the Middle East conflict, with local officials saying residents are not affected by electricity cost increases.

stories.title: Police bust 'maze-like' drug tunnel to Morocco in Spanish exclave of Ceuta
stories.summary: Spain's close ties with Latin America and proximity to Morocco, a top cannabis producer, make it a key entry point for drugs into Europe.

stories.title: Slovenian women keep centuries-old ‘drsanke’ Easter egg art alive
stories.summary: In southern Slovenia, women revive ‘drsanke’, carving intricate Easter eggs in Bela Krajina, turning simple shells into lasting handmade gifts.

stories.title: Syrian president discusses Iran war with British PM on first visit to London since fall of al-Assad
stories.summary: Ahmed al-Sharaa has managed to build relations with Western governments and made several overseas trips, including to the United States, France and Germany.

stories.title: Fire at chemical plant in Russia’s Nizhnekamsk leaves three dead
stories.summary: Footage shows thick smoke rising over an industrial site in Nizhnekamsk, western Russia.

stories.title: Iran has 'necessary will' to end war but seeking guarantees, Pezeshkian tells EU
stories.summary: US Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth said that the next days of the Iran war will be "decisive" while refusing to rule out US ground forces playing a role in the conflict.

stories.title: Palm Sunday bird sellers in Mexico City cling to fading tradition
stories.summary: Palm Sunday in Mexico City still sees pajareros lining the streets with birds and flowers, but tighter laws and criticism are shrinking the tradition.

stories.title: US journalist kidnapped in Baghdad and security forces pursuing captors, Iraqi officials say
stories.summary: It was not immediately clear if the kidnapping was related to the ongoing war, but Iran-backed militias in Iraq have launched regular attacks on US facilities in the country.

stories.title: Strikes hit southern Lebanese village, damage buildings and vehicles
stories.summary: Israeli strikes hit a village in southern Lebanon, damaging buildings and emergency vehicles as rescue teams responded

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
stories.summary: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

stories.title: 'A million things could go wrong' - why seizing Iran's uranium would be so risky for the US
stories.summary: Seizing the stockpile would be one of the "most complicated special operations in history", a former defence official tells the BBC.

stories.title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families
stories.summary: People in Iran describe angry scenes and tense relationships as rifts open up over the war.

stories.title: Judge temporarily halts construction of Trump's White House ballroom
stories.summary: The Republican-appointed judge ruled that Trump was a "steward" of the White House, not the owner.

stories.title: 'Moonshots' and merch - what it's like to be on Florida's Space Coast ahead of Artemis launch
stories.summary: Huge crowds are gathering around the Kennedy Space Centre to see the launch of the historic moon mission.

stories.title: Billion-plus people, three million officials, 33 questions - India begins huge census
stories.summary: The two-phase exercise - the world’s most ambitious - will see three million officials count every Indian.

stories.title: At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack
stories.summary: Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s breadbasket Artibonite region, significantly more than official estimates, a human rights group has said. Police initially reported 16 dead and 10 injured, while a preliminary report from civil protection authorities suggested 17 had died and 19 were wounded. Continue reading...

stories.title: Sad faces all round as Bolivia’s clowns protest over decree threatening their livelihoods
stories.summary: Clowns in Bolivia are upset by mandate that stops schools hosting events from which they earn a living Dozens of clowns have marched through the streets of Bolivia’s capital to protest against a government decree that limits extracurricular activities in schools, threatening their livelihoods. Wearing full face paint and their signature red noses, the clowns gathered on Monday in front of the ministry of education in La Paz to oppose a decree published in February.
